behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode is at module handoff: three source clients -> report assembler -> summary input payload. A unit test of one formatter function can miss dropped/miswired fields between modules. System was considered but rejected because behavior only requires verifying composed in-process payload content, not real network/subprocess/OS effects. Agentic was rejected because this behavior checks pre-generation input completeness, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real data-collection modules to the report assembly path with stubs for the three source APIs returning distinct sentinel values. Intercept the exact object/string passed into summary-generation and assert each source’s sentinel data is present in the correct section.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module error propagation and fallback rendering (source adapter failure -> orchestrator -> report output). Unit is too low because the failure often appears in wiring/exception translation boundaries. System was considered but rejected: behavior text only requires handling an unavailable source, which can be reproduced by a stub raising timeout/HTTP errors without real network. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because correctness criterion is fallback control flow, not model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Run the report pipeline with two source stubs returning data and one stub throwing an availability exception. Assert execution completes, report output exists, and only the failed source section contains `data unavailable` while other sections still include real values.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: The behavior explicitly targets semantic faithfulness of generated narrative vs input values, which depends on real model generation quality/judgment. Deterministic stubs or mocked model responses can always pass and hide hallucination/omission failures. Workflow was considered but rejected because this is a single model-quality check, not multiple agentic steps chained together.
behaviors.test_description: Use a fixed dataset with known key metrics/issues/deploy events and run the real summary-generation model multiple times. Score outputs for factual consistency against the input (key values present, no contradictory numbers). Fail if consistency drops below threshold or critical values are misstated/omitted.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary includes real external email delivery and inbox receipt semantics (network, provider acceptance, message routing). Integration fakes can miss real SMTP/API contract issues, auth, formatting/encoding quirks, or delivery failures. Agentic was considered but rejected because the behavior’s pass condition is delivery-to-inbox correctness, not evaluating model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the full scheduled-report command against real/staging source endpoints and real email delivery infrastructure targeting a test mailbox. Verify one delivered email arrives to configured recipient, with expected subject/body sections and correct attachment/format as applicable.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is orchestration across local components: send failure handling, filesystem persistence, and retry state across runs. This can be caught by wiring modules with a failing email client stub plus real local filesystem. System was considered but rejected because behavior text does not require validating real provider/network semantics, only unreachable-error handling and next-run retry logic. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified since no model-quality dependence is required.
behaviors.test_description: Run cycle 1 with email client stub raising unreachable error; assert report is persisted to local retry file/store. Run cycle 2 (simulated next schedule) with email client succeeding; assert persisted report is retried and file/state is cleared after successful send.
